review_title: Logic Monitor quickly becoming a complete observability platform
description: It has always been good but again we are reevaluating our willingness to attach such a large price tag to our monitoring. Its regrettable because we are deeply imbedded in Logic Monitor and much of our processes will need to be refactored with a new solution.
author: William G.
pros: Flexible data sources is probably in my opinion the greatest feature of LM. Not only can you easily modify existing ones but write your own to monitor really anything that has an IP.
cons: The incremental increase in cost is starting to push us to start look at doing some/all of our monitoring with other tooling. Additionally, the kubernetes cost model is encouraging us to bake monitoring software into our clusters and remove those clusters from Logic Monitor.

review_title: Logic Monitor - A GREAT solution for monitoring Infrastructure!
description: It has been positive and we are glad we have it! It did not take long to add the servers we want to monitor, and we are enjoying all the options to monitor specific aspects we were not able to monitor before with our previous product.
author: Andrew I.
pros: The flexibility, the accuracy, the in depth things that can be checked on monitored with. There are many different type of alerts that seem limited by the users imagination. We monitor such infrastructure metrics such as CPU/Mem/Disk/Network/IO. The scripting is extremely powerful.
cons: The learning curve is EXTREMELY steep! But that comes with the fact that the more flexibility a tool has, the more complicated it can be. The tool does not have the functionality of auto discovery, be it active or decommed servers. The script writing is a bit difficult to learn, but you can at least try to use the wizard that is built in.

review_title: Great look and feel, but complex for custom stats
description: The product really does do what its meant to. I did find their billing system difficult though and could never get the credit card info to display.
author: Pete E.
pros: LogicMonitor can capture a wide variety of stats from endpoints. It is also easy to navigate and quick to respond.
cons: It was incredibly complucated to do custom reports and charts. There were specific guides on their site for a variety of systems but I needed something out of the box.

description: My experience with LogicMonitor was that we had accurate monitoring, which helped keep our infrastructure running.
author: Tamer B.
pros: The ability to monitor VMs and list the alerts depends on the VM. Also, it was helping us to monitor SQL
cons: The UI was old, and there is room to improve the user experience in the silences.

review_title: Decent product at a fairly budget friendly price
author: Craig M.
pros: This is a good product for easy NOC style monitoring of your server and network infrastructure. You can even embed apps such as security cameras into your NOC board. Alerting works well and allows for escalation stages and different paths/types of alerts. The history information for disks, CPU, and memory usage have helped track down issues and it can predict upcoming usage trends. The support you get from their team has always been above par and probably the best support from any product we have purchased.
cons: The available widgets for their dashboard could use some visual additions and maybe an overhaul. I have seen some cool looking boards though if you put the time and effort in. They need more updated and modern looking examples in their gallery.

review_title: LogicMonitor NPM/alert meets all requirements with all Network Engineering and NOC
description: Overall, it's a great cloud-based tool that continues to develop the product.
author: Travis H.
pros: Meets all our requirements with NPM and Alertings, easy to setup and add devices. The cloud dashboard is easy to use.
cons: The hardest part was setting up a triage of alerts during business and non-business hours. It takes time to figure out the best way to handle spam-alerts due with a single device event that affects other devices in the stream.

description: We've had a wonderful experience with LogicMonitor overall. Easy to setup and use, provides timely alerts and good monitoring data overall. It supports all of the devices we need it to and their support is great.
author: Philip B.
pros: LogicMonitor is an easy to use operational monitoring platform. We were able to quickly deploy it across our entire envirorment.
cons: There are a few quickrs to the LogicMonitor UI. The alerts tab for the past few years has a different UI than the rest of the product. It's not a huge deal but at some point Logic needs to address that.

review_title: One of the best Monitoring Tool
description: All are good the overall features are good
author: vinay kumar reddy K.
pros: Overall features are good especially the grouping and alert ruling features are good
cons: Sometimes it get slow in the portal. It should have an application rathet than web app

review_title: A great IT infrastructure management system.
description: Use of the tool has ensured unified visibility across all our systems. The product has also provided us with real-time alerts incase of an usual activities.
author: Esami Allan J.
pros: Ability of the software to easily connect with other tools. Effectiveness of the software in alerts monitoring and notifications management. Ease in implementation and setting up.
cons: The software has very limited alerts options and complex network traffic analysis. Support services of the software are also not so reliable.

review_title: LogicMonitor Has Brilliant and Accurate Features
author: Giacomo M.
pros: It is easy to monitor our IT environments using this powerful platform. LogicMonitor has exceptional capabilities that make work easier for us.
cons: LogicMonitor has been brilliant since we deployed it in our company. The features of LogicMonitor are exceptional.

review_title: LogicMonitor Is a Powerful and Reliable Solution
description: My experience with LogicMonitor is amazing.
author: Paul V.
pros: LogicMonitor has been an exceptional alerting and monitoring solution. I am impressed by the amazing capabilities of LogicMonitor.
cons: LogicMonitor has been functioning effectively. LogicMonitor is the best.

review_title: Great combination of function and flexibility
description: Our goals were to deploy a product that would provide a single status view of our infrastructure, help us identify which teams to engage when issues occur, and utilize Saas options from our chosen vendor. It took some time and work, but Logicmonitor was the product we needed to meet our monitoring goals.Logicmonitor helps us identify problems quickly, and see when recovery is complete. Monitoring data history has been extremely useful to conduct post mortems, and predict where resources might be better deployed.Dashboards have allowed us to share pertinent information with non technical parts of the business, so they can see when problems exist without needing to call.Overall, a great product that helps our business function.
author: Tim L.
pros: Provided datasources cover most equipmentCustomizationProduct SupportMature product
cons: Reporting not as customizable as desired

review_title: LogicMonitor from a System Administrator View
author: Adam H.
pros: I like how it is very agnostic with the platforms it monitors. It can (with effort) monitor anything you want. Their customer service is top top TOP tier. I also like its integrations and the module exchane.
cons: I feel like RCA can be approved upon. I also feel like actually creating monitors can be quite difficult if its custom.

description: My experience with LM has been very good. We're currently in the process of revamping the tool in our infrastructure and that's been going smooth.
author: David R.
pros: LM has tons of features that enable monitoring of very intricate pieces of the infrastructure.
cons: Due to there being so many intricate pieces, fine tuning everything can be time consume and a little difficult to get correct without learning the tool inside and out.

description: No real issues, like the product, but tuning alerts is cumbersome
author: ed C.
pros: Ease of use, easily tune alerts, no need to add datasources as the ones we need are pre populated
cons: Sometimes too noisy, alot of work tuning

description: Great experience.
author: Oliver H.
pros: Easy to add new devices. SNMP profiles preconfigured for most popular manufacturers. Great dashboarding and customization.
cons: A little tricky to write your own custom monitoring modules independently. However, good product support and knowledgeable deployment success teams.

description: Hello, I will try to bullet out a list of pros and cons below. My overall impression of Logic Monitor is a positive one. I have been happy with the scale at which we are able to adopt the systems. In itself Logic Monitor is a great service but I'd like to see more invested to integrate with other popular monitoring services like PagerDuty, etc. A few minor inefficiencies in the UI, but nothing major for us. . and by the way your support is great! Pros Exceptionally consistent! It's easy to be confident that all our alerts across the board are consistent unless set otherwise manually. -Flexible. I think the tool has just the right level of flexibility to it so that we can organize alerts and hosts in a manner that effectively aligns with our business. Visibility. Implementing the tool right as really increased our team's visibility to our overall health and performance. It's been a great win. Cons Pagerduty integration. There is a lot that we use Logic Monitor for but it will never be our ONLY monitoring tool, as a result we need a service like pager duty as a central hub to manage and maintain our alert streams. It is incredibly cumbersome today to not have resolutions close out alerts in PagerDuty and also to not have alerts that are acknowledged in pager duty acknowledge in Logic Monitor. This is the most important con to us right now. Transparency. There are some nice features that help you see the various alerts whether it's the alerts tab counters (that show on the main page) or the way hosts highlight when they have alerts associated. I would like to see more detail on those features, for example if there are 13 critical alerts, it would be nice to know how many are already Ack'd without clicking into the alerts tab and searching for those alerts. It would also be ideal if you could highlight the "hosts" on the hosts tab with the color of severity of their alerts. We spend a lot of time in the host view and being able to see a host highlighted yellow, orange, or red would be helpful to faster decision making. The alerts dashboard is not clear for hosts that are in SDT. I would actually prefer not to see those hosts in the alerts totals on the alerts tab, or potentially make that configurable. It's confusing to go to that tab to see alerts for hosts that are in SDT and don't actually need immediate response, those and Ack'd errors could almost be their own tab. Thanks for a great service and continued product development!
author: Andy D.
Dave A.
pros: I love how I can easily see the health of a system or application and get alerted when thresholds are met
cons: Sometimes it can be hard finding what you're looking for when you have a lot of systems and applications

review_title: Great application from the ground up
description: If you built this from the ground up it is great. If you walk into an environment with it implemented it's not fun to learn.
author: Jason B.
pros: Very customizable. Can do whatever you want.
cons: Very complicated software. Not for the faint of heart.

description: Overall things have been great. Account manager has been awesome and product has continued to improve over time.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Ease of use and all the pre-built data-points are awesome!
cons: Would be nice to have a better way to organize application/device dependencies on a visual display like a network path or rack layout NOC display.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It really does what it has to do - it monitors almost everything that you could think of, it has a lot of datasources for almost any software or hardware, it has flexible notification system, and, finally, useful dashboards.
cons: Learning curve is pretty high. Some datasources are too basic. Personally i would like to have software/hardware assessment module in LM.

description: The program cut down the amount of time engineers spent checking resource management of vcenter.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: INTEGRATIONS! Logic Monitor allows us to monitor every aspect of our customer environments. We use the connectwise integration top open and close tickets.
cons: It requires a lot of tuning for alerts that occur often but are still important.

description: it's a good tool to monitor systems and get notifications for critical issues.
author: Leon F.
pros: dashboard is clear and ease to use. data is accurate.
cons: I don't really use too much and so far I didn't see the cons.

description: The first thing I do when I log into my computer is open LogicMonitor. LogicMonitor helps to make my job easier.
author: Matthew K.
pros: Reports and the automation that can be built into it.
cons: The new UI.

description: Monitoring system in Azure
author: Craig H.
pros: Has lots of features, can access most cloud based system, flexible
cons: Can be difficult to use, due to having so many features.
